Governor-General
15th February, 2023.
SAINT CHRISTOPHER AND NEVIS
No. 2 of 2023
AN ACT to amend the Criminal Records (Rehabilitation of Offenders) Act, Cap. 3.25.
[Published 23rd February 2023, Official Gazette No. 11 of 2023.]
BE IT ENACTED by the King's Most Excellent Majesty, by and with the advice and
consent of the National Assembly of Saint Christopher and Nevis, and by the authority of
the same as follows:
1. Short title.
This Act may be cited as the Criminal Records (Rehabilitation of Offenders) (Amendment)
Act, 2023.
2. Interpretation.
In this Act,
“Act” means the Criminal Records (Rehabilitation of Offenders) Act, Cap. 3.25.
3. Amendment of section 2.
The Act is amended in section 2 by adding the following definitions in the correct
alphabetical order
“ “cannabis” has the meaning assigned to it in the Drugs (Prevention & Abatement
of the Misuse and Abuse of Drugs) Act, Cap. 9.08;
“cannabis resin” means the separate resin, whether crude or purified, obtained from
any plant of the genus cannabis;”.
4. Amendment of section 4.
The Act is amended in section 4 subsection (8) by
(a) deleting the word “or” in paragraph (a)
(b) replacing the expression “.” in paragraph (b) with the expression “; or”; and

2
Criminal Records (Rehabilitation of Offenders) (Amendment) Act, 2023 - 2.
(c) adding a new paragraph (c) as follows
“(c) who is convicted under the Drugs (Prevention & Abatement of the Misuse and
Abuse of Drugs) Act, Cap. 9.08 of an offence
(i) involving the cultivation of small amounts of cannabis plants not exceeding
five cannabis plants; or
(ii) involving the cultivation of cannabis plants and the sentence imposed was
imprisonment for a term not exceeding three years.”.
LANEIN K BLANCHETTE
Speaker
Passed by the National Assembly this 8th day of February, 2023.
TREVLYN STAPLETON
Clerk of the National Assembly
GOVERNMENT PRINTERY
ST KITTS W I
